summary: Executed backend pytest regression for required APIs and mobile-first Expo UI integration flows on public preview URL. Core user flows are working end-to-end.

ui_bugs.issue: Several interactive elements are missing testID attributes (tabs, palette cards/chips, save cards, stripe-mode buttons, steppers), making automation brittle and violating testability requirement.

issues: Munker effect toggle appears partially clipped/overflowing at the right edge in the control header row.

action_items: Add testID to all interactive components, especially tab items and list cards.
Fix mobile layout overflow in Munker filter header row so toggle remains fully visible on 375px width.
Optional maintainability: split /app/frontend/app/index.tsx into smaller screen/component modules.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/app/index.tsx is 1000+ lines (>700 guideline), increasing maintenance risk.
Current API base key uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL; align env key naming consistently across docs/code to avoid configuration drift.
seed_data_creation: Created render test records via POST /api/renders during testing (titles prefixed TEST_ where applicable in backend pytest).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use public URL from frontend/.env (EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL). Core flow verified: render create -> gallery/palette load -> saves load back into render. Focus retest on testID coverage and mobile layout clipping after fixes.
rca_of_issue: The clipped toggle appears due constrained row width in the control header with long left-side text + fixed-size button. Missing testIDs stem from partial instrumentation only on a subset of interactive components.
